title: function-typed prop (State pattern, per-state pin)
manager: A state can pin a function-typed prop — behavior attached per state — which is the State pattern made native: entering a state selects an implementation, and the machine's behavior changes with its state without a pile of mode-checking conditionals. The classic "the object behaves differently depending on which mode it's in" modeled as exactly what it is, a function that comes with the state.
cs: function-typed property, pinnable per state; the State pattern; backed by fn types (W6.26) and their portable identity (W6.27); fsl#1026/#1027 lineage.
